J1.basis: GSC shows the 12 legacy posts pull thousands of impressions on email-cleanup queries but near-zero clicks (positions 42–58) — wrong audience for a meeting-assistant SaaS
J1.rationale: Explicitly identifies the InboxZap→Lumina pivot, treats the inbox-cleanup GSC history as legacy/wrong-audience demand, and frames the fixes (bridge notes, new meeting cluster) around the live meeting-assistant reality. Loses a point only because the pivot evidence is asserted rather than shown with the actual export rows.

J2.basis: Kept the URLs and content (don't torch existing equity/backlinks) but added a bridge note + internal link to /features on each
J2.rationale: One blanket disposition (keep-all + bridge) applied uniformly to all 12 posts, loosely grounded in an aggregate GSC figure ('thousands of impressions... positions 42–58') but never walked asset-by-asset with real per-page impressions or any harvest/redirect/sunset differentiation.

J3.basis: Kept the URLs and content... redirecting that traffic toward the actual product... added 4 new posts targeting Lumina's real keyword space
J3.rationale: Explicitly avoids both failure modes — preserves legacy equity via bridge/internal links rather than torching it, and builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine — though the transition is stated more as a one-time fix than a sequenced plan.

GSC shows the 12 legacy posts pull thousands of impressions on email-cleanup queries but near-zero clicks (positions 42–58) — wrong audience for a meeting-assistant SaaS
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, treats GSC inbox impressions as legacy/wrong-audience demand, and frames both the technical fixes and the new meeting-assistant content cluster around the current product reality.

J2.basis: Kept the URLs and content (don't torch existing equity/backlinks) but added a bridge note + internal link to `/features` on each
J2.rationale: Applies one blanket disposition — keep-all-plus-bridge — to every legacy post rather than differentiating by asset; cites GSC only in aggregate (impressions, positions 42–58) with no per-post impressions/positions or harvest/redirect/sunset tiering, so it clears the 'no data at all' floor but stops short of asset-by-asset reasoning.

J3.basis: Kept the URLs and content (don't torch existing equity/backlinks) but added a bridge note... redirecting that traffic toward the actual product... added 4 new posts targeting Lumina's real keyword space... this is where new organic traffic has to come from
J3.rationale: Cleanly avoids both failure modes — preserves legacy equity while bridging it to the product, and builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine — explicitly acknowledging the legacy audience is wrong without torching its authority.

J1.basis: The site had rebranded from InboxZap (email cleanup tool) to Lumina (AI meeting assistant), but the SEO/technical foundation never caught up... GSC shows the 12 legacy posts pull thousands of impressions on email-cleanup queries but near-zero clicks... wrong audience for a meeting-assistant SaaS
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, tags legacy GSC demand as the wrong audience, and frames every fix around the meeting-assistant reality (new clusters, product internal links, rewritten metadata).

J2.basis: Kept the URLs and content (don't torch existing equity/backlinks) but added a bridge note + internal link to /features on each

J3.basis: Kept the URLs and content (don't torch existing equity/backlinks)... added 4 new posts targeting Lumina's real keyword space... GSC shows zero visibility for anything meeting/transcription-related — this is where new organic traffic has to come from
